{{神社 |名称 = みか神社 |画像 = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 10 |緯度秒 = 33.5 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 09 |経度秒 = 25.9 |祭神 = 櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命 |神体 = |社格 = 旧県社、式内（小）社 |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月13日 |神事 = }} '''みか神社'''は、[[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町]]の[[利根川]]の沿いにある神社。旧[[那賀郡]]の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。武蔵国那賀郡「みか神社」の式内小社で、旧社格は県社。
== 由緒 == 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には古墳が多数残っており、古くからみか神社の「みか」は、酒作りで使う大甕が起源となっている。
== 交通 == * JR八高線松久駅から徒歩30分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{デフォルトソート:ひめみやしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:美里町]] [[Category:児玉郡]]

10月15日 |神事 = }} '''みか神社'''は、[[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]の[[利根川]]の沿いにある[[神社]]。旧[[那賀郡]]の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。[[武蔵国]]那賀郡「みか神社」の式内小社で、旧社格は県社。 == 由緒 == 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には[[古墳]]が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、[[酒造|酒作り]]で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「菱＋玉」と表記されている。なお、「菱＋玉」は、社号のみ使われる文字となっている。 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|本殿 '''祭神''' * [[スサノオ|櫛御気野命]]（くしみけぬのみこと） :　※素戔嗚尊（すさのうのみこと）の別名 * [[大物主|櫛瓺玉命]]（しくしみかたまのみこと） :　※大物主神（おおものぬしのかみ）の別名 '''社格''' * [[享保]]8年（1723年） 正一位 * 明治元年（1872年） 村社 * 明治37年（1909年） 県社 == 摂末社 == 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|摂社群 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|神明神社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|稲荷社 == 交通 == * JR[[八高線]][[松久駅]]から徒歩30分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:みかしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:美里町 (埼玉県)]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019-09-23}} {{記事名の制約|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]]神社 (美里町)}} {{神社 |名称 = みか神社 |画像 = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 10 |緯度秒 = 33.5 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 09 |経度秒 = 25.9 |祭神 = 櫛御気野命
櫛瓺玉命 |神体 = |社格 = 旧県社
式内（小）社 |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = 木造流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月13日
10月15日 |神事 = }} '''みか神社'''は、[[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]の[[利根川]]の沿いにある[[神社]]。旧[[那珂郡 (埼玉県)|那賀郡]]の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。[[武蔵国]]那賀郡「みか神社」の式内小社で、旧社格は県社。 == 由緒 == 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には[[古墳]]が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、[[酒造|酒作り]]で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」（菱＋玉）と表記されている。なお、「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」は、社号のみ使われる文字となっている。 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|本殿 '''祭神''' [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|150px|thumb|{{center|拝殿の扁額}}]] * [[スサノオ|櫛御気野命]]（くしみけぬのみこと） :　※素戔嗚尊（すさのうのみこと）の別名 * [[大物主|櫛瓺玉命]]（しくしみかたまのみこと） :　※大物主神（おおものぬしのかみ）の別名 '''社格''' * [[享保]]8年（1723年） 正一位 * 明治元年（1872年） 村社 * 明治37年（1909年） 県社 == 摂末社 == 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|摂社群 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|神明神社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|稲荷社 == 交通 == * JR[[八高線]][[松久駅]]から徒歩30分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:みかしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:美里町 (埼玉県)]] [[Category:Unicodeに存在しない文字]]

{{出典の明記|date=2019-09-23}} {{記事名の制約|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]]神社 (美里町)}} {{神社 |名称 = みか神社 |画像 = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 10 |緯度秒 = 33.5 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 09 |経度秒 = 25.9 |祭神 = 櫛御気野命

revision.comment: フューチャー がページ「[[みか神社 (美里町)]]」をリダイレクトの「[[みか神社]]」に移動しました: [[ノート:美里町]]における合意に基づく。曖昧さ回避が不要なため。

10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=みか神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|拝殿の扁額}}]] '''みか神社'''は、[[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]の[[利根川]]沿いにある[[神社]]。旧[[那珂郡 (埼玉県)|那賀郡]]の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。[[武蔵国]]那賀郡「みか神社」の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には[[古墳]]が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、[[酒造|酒作り]]で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」（菱＋玉）と表記されている。なお、「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」は、社号のみ使われる文字となっている。 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|本殿 '''祭神''' * [[スサノオ|櫛御気野命]]（くしみけぬのみこと） :　※素戔嗚尊（すさのうのみこと）の別名 * [[大物主|櫛瓺玉命]]（くしみかたまのみこと） :　※大物主神（おおものぬしのかみ）の別名 '''社格''' * [[享保]]8年（1723年） 正一位 * 明治元年（1872年） 村社 * 明治37年（1909年） 県社 == 摂末社 == 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|摂社群 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|神明神社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|稲荷社 == 交通 == * JR[[八高線]][[松久駅]]から徒歩30分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:みかしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の歴史]] [[Category:Unicodeに存在しない文字]]

10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=みか神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|拝殿の扁額}}]] '''みか神社'''は、[[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]の[[利根川]]沿いにある[[神社]]。旧[[那珂郡 (埼玉県)|那賀郡]]の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。[[武蔵国]]那賀郡「みか神社」の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には[[古墳]]が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、[[酒造|酒作り]]で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」（菱＋玉）と表記される。 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|本殿 '''祭神''' * [[スサノオ|櫛御気野命]]（くしみけぬのみこと） :　※素戔嗚尊（すさのうのみこと）の別名 * [[大物主|櫛瓺玉命]]（くしみかたまのみこと） :　※大物主神（おおものぬしのかみ）の別名 '''社格''' * [[享保]]8年（1723年） 正一位 * 明治元年（1872年） 村社 * 明治37年（1909年） 県社 == 摂末社 == 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|摂社群 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|神明神社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|稲荷社 == 交通 == * JR[[八高線]][[松久駅]]から徒歩30分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:みかしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の歴史]] [[Category:Unicodeに存在しない文字]]

{{nihongo|'''Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)'''|みか神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)'''|みか神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)'''|みか神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)'''|みか神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama) | native_name = みか神社 | image = Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama) | coordinates = {{coord|36.176117|139.157114}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)'''|みか神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)'''|みか神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{出典の明記|date=2019-09-23}} {{記事名の制約|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]]神社}} {{神社 |名称 = みか神社 |画像 = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]広木１ |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 櫛御気野命
櫛瓺玉命 |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[県社]]
[[式内社|式内（小）社]] |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = 木造流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月13日
10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=みか神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|拝殿の扁額}}]] '''みか神社'''は、[[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]の[[利根川]]沿いにある[[神社]]。旧[[那珂郡 (埼玉県)|那賀郡]]の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。[[武蔵国]]那賀郡「みか神社」の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には[[古墳]]が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、[[酒造|酒作り]]で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」（菱＋玉）と表記される。 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|本殿 '''祭神''' * [[スサノオ|櫛御気野命]]（くしみけぬのみこと） :　※素戔嗚尊（すさのうのみこと）の別名 * [[大物主|櫛瓺玉命]]（くしみかたまのみこと） :　※大物主神（おおものぬしのかみ）の別名 '''社格''' * [[享保]]8年（1723年） 正一位 * 明治元年（1872年） 村社 * 明治37年（1909年） 県社 == 摂末社 == 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|摂社群 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|神明神社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|稲荷社 == 交通 == * JR[[八高線]][[松久駅]]から徒歩30分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:みかしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の歴史]] [[Category:Unicodeに存在しない文字]]

拝殿 |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]広木１ |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 櫛御気野命

10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=みか神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|拝殿の扁額}}]] '''みか神社'''は、[[埼玉県]][[児玉郡]][[美里町 (埼玉県)|美里町]]の[[利根川]]沿いにある[[神社]]。旧[[那珂郡 (埼玉県)|那賀郡]]の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。[[武蔵国]]那賀郡「みか神社」の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には[[古墳]]が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、[[酒造|酒作り]]で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」（菱＋玉）と表記される。 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|鳥居 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|本殿 '''祭神''' * [[スサノオ|櫛御気野命]]（くしみけぬのみこと） :　※素戔嗚尊（すさのうのみこと）の別名 * [[大物主|櫛瓺玉命]]（くしみかたまのみこと） :　※大物主神（おおものぬしのかみ）の別名 '''社格''' * [[享保]]8年（1723年） 正一位 * 明治元年（1872年） 村社 * 明治37年（1909年） 県社 == 摂末社 == 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|摂社群 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|神明神社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|稲荷社 == 交通 == * JR[[八高線]][[松久駅]]から徒歩30分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の歴史]] [[Category:Unicodeに存在しない文字]]

{{nihongo|'''Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)'''|みか神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

content == {{出典の明記|date=2019-09-23}} {{記事名の制約|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]]神社}} {{神社 |名称 = みか神社 |画像 = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|美里町 (埼玉県)|Q924129|美里町}}広木１ |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 櫛御気野命
櫛瓺玉命 |神体 = |社格 = 旧{{jalink|県社}}
{{jalink|式内社||式内（小）社}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = 木造流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月13日
10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=みか神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|拝殿の扁額}}]] '''みか神社'''は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|美里町 (埼玉県)|Q924129|美里町}}の{{jalink|利根川|Q177452}}沿いにある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|那珂郡 (埼玉県)|Q11642797|那賀郡}}の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}那賀郡「みか神社」の{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 由緒 === 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、{{jalink|酒造||酒作り}}で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」（菱＋玉）と表記される。 {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|櫛御気野命}}（くしみけぬのみこと） :　※素戔嗚尊（すさのうのみこと）の別名 * {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|櫛瓺玉命}}（くしみかたまのみこと） :　※大物主神（おおものぬしのかみ）の別名 '''社格''' * {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}8年（1723年） 正一位 * 明治元年（1872年） 村社 * 明治37年（1909年） 県社 === 摂末社 === 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) {{jalink|式内社||式内（小）社}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = 木造流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月13日
10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=みか神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|拝殿の扁額}}]] '''みか神社'''は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|児玉郡|Q938038}}{{jalink|美里町 (埼玉県)|Q924129|美里町}}の{{jalink|利根川|Q177452}}沿いにある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。旧{{jalink|那珂郡 (埼玉県)|Q11642797|那賀郡}}の総鎮守。祭神は櫛御気野命、櫛瓺玉命の2柱。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}那賀郡「みか神社」の{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 由緒 === 記録や伝承もないため、創建時期は不詳。周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}が多数残っており、利根川とその支流に囲まれ、古くから稲作が盛んに行われている。みか神社の「みか」は、{{jalink|酒造||酒作り}}で使う大甕が起源となっている。社頭掲示板（作成者:埼玉県、作成日:昭和58年3月）より社号の「みか」は、漢字では「瓺」＋「[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]」（菱＋玉）と表記される。 明治37年（1909年） 県社 === 摂末社 === 以下の社は、社殿の周りに囲い屋根がある。 * 産泰社、諏訪社 * 天神天満宮、神明社、蚕影社 * 山王神社、松尾社 以下の社は、石祠群 * 古峯山、鹿島社、稲荷社、愛宕社、天手長社、大雷社、天照太神・少名彦名神・倉稲魂命・大己貴命・埴安媛命、弁天社 File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) content == {{Citation needed|date=2019-09-23}} {{Article title restriction|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]] Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mika Shrine |Image = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = Hiroki 1, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}{{jalink|Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|Misato Town}} |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha||Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located along the {{jalink|Tone River|Q177452}} in {{jalink|Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|Misato Town}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{jalink|Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|Naka District}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{jalink|sake brewing||sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{jalink|Matsuhisa Station|Q845010}} on the JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mika Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の歴史]] [[Category:Unicodeに存在しない文字]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

content == {{Citation needed|date=2019-09-23}} {{Article title restriction|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]] Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mika Shrine |Image = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = Hiroki 1, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}{{jalink|Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|Misato Town}} |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha||Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located along the {{jalink|Tone River|Q177452}} in {{jalink|Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|Misato Town}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{jalink|Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|Naka District}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{jalink|sake brewing||sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{jalink|Matsuhisa Station|Q845010}} on the JR {{jalink|Hachikō Line|Q1372071}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mika Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mika Shrine (Misato town, Saitama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県美里町の歴史]] [[Category:Unicodeに存在しない文字]] [[Category:pages]]

Worship hall |Location = Hiroki 1, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}{{jalink|Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|Misato Town}} |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha||Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located along the {{jalink|Tone River|Q177452}} in {{jalink|Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|Misato Town}}, {{jalink|Kodama District|Q938038}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{jalink|Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|Naka District}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{jalink|sake brewing||sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from Worship hall |Location = Hiroki 1, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}{{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town}} |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|WD=Q177452}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|WD=Q11642797|lt=Naka District}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing||WD=|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Matsuhisa Station|Q845010|WD=Q845010}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mika Shrine}} == Categories October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|WD=Q177452}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|WD=Q11642797|lt=Naka District}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|WD=Q11642797|lt=Naka District}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Matsuhisa Station|Q845010|WD=Q845010}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|WD=Q177452|1=Tone River}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|WD=Q11642797|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Matsuhisa Station|Q845010|WD=Q845010|1=Matsuhisa Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}} === Notes === {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|WD=Q177452|1=Tone River}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|WD=Q11642797|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Matsuhisa Station|Q845010|WD=Q845010|1=Matsuhisa Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}} === Notes === {{Citation needed|date=2019-09-23}} {{Article title restriction|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]] Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mika Shrine |Image = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = Hiroki 1, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}} |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|WD=Q177452|1=Tone River}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|WD=Q11642797|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Matsuhisa Station|Q845010|WD=Q845010|1=Matsuhisa Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}} === Notes === Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|WD=Q177452|1=Tone River}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|WD=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|WD=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|WD=Q11642797|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Matsuhisa Station|Q845010|WD=Q845010|1=Matsuhisa Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|WD=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}} === Notes === Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2019-09-23}} {{Article title restriction|瓺[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|20px]] Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mika Shrine |Image = [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = Hiroki 1, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|qid=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|qid=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}} |Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates DMS|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|qid=Q177452|1=Tone River}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|qid=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|qid=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|qid=Q11642797|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Torii.jpg|Torii gate File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato-machi) Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Honden.jpg|Main hall '''Enshrined deities''' * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Susanoo-no-Mikoto * {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} (Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto) : ※ An alternate name for Ōmononushi-no-Kami '''Shrine rank''' * Kyōhō 8 (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Keidasha.jpg|Group of subsidiary shrines File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Shinmyou-jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Inari-sha.jpg|Inari Shrine === Access === * About a 30-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Matsuhisa Station|Q845010|qid=Q845010|1=Matsuhisa Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|qid=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}} === Notes === {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Wooden nagare-zukuri style |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13
October 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=10|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=9|lon_sec=25.9|label=Mika Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|10|33.5|N|139|9|25.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Mika-jinja(Misato,Saitama) Hengaku.jpg|265px|thumb|{{center|Dedication plaque of the worship hall}}]] '''Mika Shrine''' is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located along the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|qid=Q177452|1=Tone River}} in {{ill|lt=Misato Town, Saitama|Q924129|qid=Q924129|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Saitama (town)}}, {{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|qid=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the tutelary shrine of the former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Saitama|Q11642797|qid=Q11642797|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. The enshrined deities are Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto and Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto. It is listed as the “Mika Shrine” of Naka District in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === History === As there are no records or legends, the date of founding is unknown. Many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} burial mounds remain in the surrounding area, which is enclosed by the Tone River and its tributaries, and rice cultivation has flourished here since ancient times. The “mika” in Mika Shrine is said to originate from large jars used in {{ill|lt=sake brewing|UNKNOWN|lt=sake making}}.Shrine notice board (prepared by: Saitama Prefecture, date: March 1983) The shrine name “mika” is written in kanji as “瓺” plus “[[File:U2ff1-ufa5e-u2ffa-u590c-u7389.svg|18px]]” (diamond + jewel). (1723): Senior First Rank * Meiji 1 (1872): Village shrine * Meiji 37 (1909): Prefectural shrine === Subsidiary shrines === The following shrines are surrounded by a roofed enclosure around the main buildings: * Santai Shrine, Suwa Shrine * Tenjin Tenmangū, Shinmei Shrine, Kokage Shrine * Sannō Shrine, Matsuo Shrine The following are groups of stone shrines: * Komagatake Shrine, Kashima Shrine, Inari Shrine, Atago Shrine, Ameno-Tenaga Shrine, Ōikazuchi Shrine, Amaterasu-Ōmikami, Sukunahikona-no-Kami, Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto, Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Haniyasuhime-no-Mikoto, Benten Shrine
